What is MDC full form in WAPDA?: MDC stands for Meter Data Controller. They are responsible for collecting, verifying, and managing consumer meter readings.
What is the basic job description of an: An MDC records monthly electricity meter readings, inspects meters for faults or tampering, and ensures data is accurately fed into the billing system.
What grade or pay scale is MDC in WAPDA?: An MDC in WAPDA typically falls under BPS-09, though this can vary slightly based on specific DISCO policies and seniority.
Who is the direct supervisor of an MDC?: The direct supervisor of an MDC is usually the Line Superintendent (LS) or the Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) of the respective subdivision.
What department does MDC work in?: MDCs work in the Operations and Revenue departments of the distribution companies (DISCOs) under WAPDA.
What is the difference between MDC and M: MDC (Meter Data Controller) is a modernized designation for Meter Reader, often involving use of digital devices and advanced data management compared to traditional manual reading.
What is the difference between MDC and S: An MDC is field staff responsible for recording readings, while an SDO (Sub-Divisional Officer) is an officer (usually an engineer) who heads the entire subdivision operations.
How many MDCs are usually assigned per s: The number of MDCs depends on the subdivision's consumer base, usually ranging from 10 to 30 MDCs per subdivision.
What is the career path after MDC in WAP: An MDC can be promoted to Meter Inspector, Commercial Assistant, and with higher education, to Revenue Officer (RO).
Can an MDC get promoted to a higher post: Yes, through departmental promotion exams and seniority, an MDC can be promoted to supervisory roles like Meter Inspector.
What is the minimum education required f: The minimum education required is typically Matriculation or Intermediate, often with a diploma in IT or related fields.
Is there a test for MDC recruitment in W: Yes, DISCOs conduct written tests (often via NTS or PTS) followed by interviews for MDC recruitment.
What subjects are tested in MDC recruitm: Tests usually cover Basic Mathematics, General Knowledge, Basic IT/Computer skills, and English.
Is computer knowledge required for MDC p: Yes, basic computer knowledge is essential as MDCs use handheld devices and digital billing systems.
What is the age limit for applying to MD: The general age limit is 18 to 30 years, though age relaxation applies as per government rules.
Is there a physical fitness requirement: Yes, physical fitness is required because the job involves extensive walking and field visits in various weather conditions.
What documents are required for MDC job: CNIC, Domicile, Educational Certificates, character certificate, and passport-size photographs are typically required.
Which authority conducts MDC recruitment: Recruitment is conducted by the respective DISCOs (like LESCO, MEPCO, FESCO) often through third-party testing agencies.
Is there a probation period for new MDC: Yes, there is usually a probation period of 1 to 2 years before the job is made permanent.
What training is given to a newly appoin: Training covers meter reading techniques, device operation, safety protocols, and consumer dealing.
What does an MDC do at the start of the: They report to the subdivision, sync their handheld devices, receive the daily route schedule, and collect any specific instructions.
What is the daily field routine of an MD: Visiting assigned areas, recording readings, taking photographs of meters, noting defects, and interacting with consumers if needed.
How many meters does an MDC read per day: An MDC typically reads between 200 to 400 meters per day depending on the density of the area.
What time does an MDC start field work?: Field work usually starts early in the morning around 8:00 AM to maximize daylight and avoid extreme afternoon heat.
What does an MDC do after completing fie: They return to the office, sync the recorded data to the main server, and report any discrepancies or faulty meters to the supervisor.
How does an MDC plan his daily meter rea: The route is pre-assigned by the Revenue Office in a specific sequence (Route or Batch number) to minimize travel time.
What is a meter reading schedule in WAPD: It is a monthly timetable defining exactly which dates specific batches or routes of meters must be read.
How does MDC submit data at end of day?: Data is submitted by connecting the handheld device (HHU) or smartphone app to the subdivision's main computer system via USB or Wi-Fi.
What happens if MDC cannot complete dail: The remaining readings must be completed the next day, and the supervisor must be informed of the delay reasons.
What records does MDC maintain daily?: They maintain logs of total meters read, locked premises, defective meters, and suspected theft cases.
How does an MDC read an analog meter cor: By noting the numbers from left to right on the dials, ignoring the last red dial which represents decimal fractions.
How does an MDC read a digital meter?: By pressing the display button to cycle through parameters and noting the active energy consumption (kWh) value.
How does an MDC read a three-phase meter: Similar to digital meters, but they may also need to record MDI (Maximum Demand Indicator) and kVARh readings for commercial/industrial consumers.
What is the correct way to note down met: Ensure the meter number matches the record, write/type the exact digits shown (excluding decimals), and take a clear photograph.
How does MDC handle a meter that is inst: They use binoculars, mobile phone zoom cameras, or safely request a ladder/stool. They must not take unsafe risks.
What if the meter reading is lower than: This indicates a meter change, dial rollover, or tampering. The MDC must flag it immediately for inspection.
How does MDC handle a spinning meter wit: They report it as a 'Defective/Blank Display' meter and take a photograph of the condition.
What does MDC do if the meter number is: They identify the meter via cross-checking the consumer name/address and report the missing or faded number plate.
How does MDC confirm the correct consume: By matching the unique meter serial number engraved on the meter with the route sheet or digital record.
What is the procedure for reading meters: Locate the centralized meter panel, ensure the meter matches the flat number via serial numbers, and read systematically.
What software does WAPDA MDC use for dat: They use specialized mobile applications developed by PITC (Power Information Technology Company) or specific DISCO HHU software.
How does MDC enter readings into the WAP: Via the smartphone application which directly uploads to the server, or manually via desktop operators if devices fail.
What is a handheld device used by MDC?: An HHU (Hand Held Unit) or a customized Android smartphone equipped with reading software and a camera.
How does MDC sync data from device to ma: By returning to the office and connecting to the local server via LAN, USB, or using cellular 4G data.
What happens if MDC device battery dies: MDCs usually carry power banks. If it completely fails, they must manually record readings in a ledger to avoid delays.
How does MDC correct a wrong entry in th: They can edit the entry in the app before syncing. Once synced, they must submit a formal correction request to the RO.
What is a route sheet in WAPDA MDC work?: A printed or digital list showing the sequence of consumers to be visited, including names, addresses, and meter numbers.
How does MDC manage offline data entry?: The app stores readings locally on the device and automatically uploads them once an internet connection is restored.
What is the deadline for MDC data submis: Data must be submitted exactly as per the billing schedule (usually between the 5th and 20th of the month) to ensure timely billing.
How does MDC handle duplicate meter entr: The software usually prevents duplicates. If found, the MDC reports the duplicate billing reference to the IT department for merging.
How does MDC calculate units consumed by: By subtracting the Previous Month's Reading from the Current Month's Reading. (Current - Previous = Consumed).
What is the formula for electricity unit: Consumed Units (kWh) = Current Reading - Previous Reading.
What is an estimated bill in WAPDA?: A bill generated based on historical average consumption when the actual reading cannot be taken (e.g., locked premises).
When does MDC apply an estimated reading: When the meter is defective, the premises are securely locked for consecutive months, or extreme weather prevents access.
How does MDC handle zero consumption rea: They verify if the premises are vacant or disconnected. If occupied, they investigate for a dead meter or bypass.
What is negative consumption and how is: When the current reading is lower than previous. Handled by checking for dial rollover (crossing 99999), meter change, or faulty entry.
How does MDC manage billing for industri: They record kWh, MDI (kW), and kVARh. These readings are critical as industrial tariffs apply peak/off-peak rates and penalties.
What is TOD metering in WAPDA billing?: Time of Day (TOD) metering charges different rates for Peak and Off-Peak hours. MDC must record both readings separately.
How does MDC handle billing for agricult: Tube well meters are read carefully. MDCs ensure the motor capacity matches the sanctioned load.
What is the difference between sanctione: Sanctioned load is the maximum power legally allowed (in kW). Actual consumption is the energy used over time (in kWh).
What are the types of meters MDC handles: Single-phase, Three-phase, TOD, MDI, AMR, and Smart Meters.
What is a single-phase meter?: A standard 220V meter used for residential properties with low power requirements (typically under 5kW).
What is a three-phase meter and who uses: A 400V meter used for large houses, commercial, or industrial properties with high loads (ACs, heavy machinery).
What is a prepaid meter and how does MDC: Prepaid meters operate on recharged credit. MDCs may occasionally audit them, but regular reading isn't required for billing.
What is a smart meter and what is MDC's: Smart meters transmit data remotely via SIM cards. MDC's role shifts to physical audits, tamper checks, and maintenance rather than manual reading.
What is an AMR meter in WAPDA?: Automated Meter Reading (AMR) meters allow remote data collection. MDCs cross-verify readings if the automated system fails.
What is the difference between electrome: Electromechanical uses a spinning disc. Electronic meters have digital LCD screens and are much more accurate and tamper-resistant.
How does MDC handle net metering for sol: They must record two values: 'Import' (units taken from WAPDA) and 'Export' (units sent to the grid from solar).
What is a CT meter and when is it used?: Current Transformer (CT) meters are used for very high industrial loads where direct measurement would melt the meter.
How does MDC read a TOD (Time of Day) me: By using the scroll button to cycle the display to explicitly note Peak kWh and Off-Peak kWh separately.
What are the consumer categories in WAPD: Domestic (Residential), Commercial, Industrial, Agricultural, Public Lighting, and Bulk Supply.
What is a domestic consumer in WAPDA?: A household connection intended purely for residential living purposes. (Tariff A-1).
What is a commercial consumer and how is: Shops and businesses. They are charged at a higher, flat commercial tariff rate (Tariff A-2) and taxes apply differently.
What is an industrial consumer category?: Factories and manufacturing plants (Tariff B). They require three-phase CT meters, TOD billing, and strict MDI limits.
What is an agricultural consumer (tube w: Farms using electricity specifically for tube wells to pump water (Tariff D). Often subsidized.
How does MDC handle government instituti: Read identically to standard meters, but billed under specific public tariffs. Disconnections require higher authority approval.
What is a bulk supply consumer?: Housing societies or large complexes that buy electricity at a single master meter and distribute it internally.
What is a street light connection and wh: Public lighting connections. MDCs read the master meters installed on specific poles, billed to the municipal corporation.
How does MDC handle mosques and religiou: Read standardly. In some regions, they may fall under specific subsidized or exempt tariffs, but accurate reading is mandatory.
What is a lifeline consumer in WAPDA?: A poor domestic consumer using less than 50 or 100 units per month continuously. They receive heavily subsidized rates.
How does MDC identify a faulty meter?: Blank screen, burnt smell, flashing error lights, stopped display despite load running, or physical damage.
What should MDC do if a meter is burnt?: Take a photo, do not touch it due to safety risks, mark it as 'Burnt' in the system, and notify the SDO immediately.
What is the procedure for a meter with b: Try the push button. If still blank, mark as 'Defective/Blank', take a photo, and report for replacement. Do not enter fake readings.
How does MDC report a slow or fast runni: Flag it as 'Suspected Defective' in the app. A testing team will be sent to calibrate and verify the meter.
What if a meter seal is broken or missin: Note 'Seal Broken' in the remarks, take a picture, and immediately inform the supervisor as it indicates potential tampering.
How does MDC document a defective meter?: Via the HHU device defect codes, accompanied by a clear photograph of the defect, and submitting a hardcopy report to the office.
What is the procedure for meter replacem: After MDC reports it, the SDO issues an MCO (Meter Change Order), line staff replace it, and the new serial is updated in the system.
How does MDC handle a meter that shows e: Note the exact error code (e.g., Err-01, Reverse Power), log it in the system, and request a technical inspection.
What happens after MDC reports a defecti: The consumer is billed on an estimated average until the meter is replaced by the maintenance team.
Who is responsible for repairing a fault: The M&T (Metering and Testing) department verifies faults, and the line staff (linemen) handle physical replacements.
How does MDC identify meter tampering?: Broken seals, drilled holes, external wires bypassing the meter, reverse rotation, or physical damage to the casing.
What is a kunda connection and how is it: A 'Kunda' is an illegal hook thrown directly onto WAPDA power lines. Identified by visible wires bypassing the official meter.
What is meter bypass and how does MDC de: When wires are routed around the meter so it doesn't record load. Detected by observing main service cables and checking if the meter blinks when load is on.
What is the reporting procedure for elec: Take photographic evidence safely, DO NOT confront the consumer, retreat to safety, and immediately inform the SDO and Anti-Theft task force.
How does MDC calculate units lost due to: MDCs do not calculate this. The Revenue Office and SDO calculate detection bills based on connected load and duration of theft.
What is an MPCR report in WAPDA?: Meter Parameters Check Report. A formal document filled out when testing a meter for accuracy or tampering.
What action is taken after MDC detects i: The task force raids the premises, removes the illegal wire, files an FIR against the culprit, and issues a heavy detection bill.
How does MDC coordinate with the anti-th: MDCs provide exact locations, photographs, and times when the theft is active, acting as the primary intelligence gatherers.
What are the legal consequences of elect: Under the Electricity Act, theft can lead to heavy fines (detection bills), immediate disconnection, and imprisonment.
Can MDC arrest someone for electricity t: No. MDCs have no police powers. They can only report. Arrests are made by the Police with WAPDA Magistrates.
How does MDC handle a consumer complaini: Politely ask them to check their current meter reading against the bill. If the meter reading matches, the bill is correct. Advise them to visit the revenue office for tariff queries.
What is the procedure for a disputed met: MDC should re-verify the reading by taking a fresh photo. If a mistake was made, a correction request is submitted to the SDO.
What does MDC do if consumer claims mete: Advise the consumer to submit a formal application for meter testing at the subdivision office. A testing team will install a check meter.
How does MDC respond to a consumer who r: MDCs do not collect cash. Inform them that non-payment will result in automatic system disconnection, and leave politely.
What documents are needed for a billing: A copy of the disputed bill, a clear picture of the current meter reading, and a written application to the SDO or RO.
How does MDC resolve a meter number mism: Photograph the installed meter, note the consumer's reference number, and submit a discrepancy report to IT to correct the database.
What if a consumer claims the meter was: Check the system's photographic record for the previous month. If the previous photo proves the reading was wrong, facilitate a correction.
How does MDC handle complaints about est: Explain that estimates occur due to locked premises or defects. Promise to input the actual reading this month to auto-adjust the next bill.
What is the process for meter testing on: Consumer pays a testing fee, M&T department removes the meter, tests it in a lab, and adjusts the bill if the meter is proven faulty.
How does MDC escalate an unresolved cons: Log it in the complaint register and direct the consumer to the Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) or XEN (Executive Engineer).
What should MDC do if premises is locked: Ring the bell. If no response, mark 'Locked' in the app, take a photo of the locked gate for proof, and schedule a revisit if possible.
How should MDC handle an aggressive dog: Safety first. Do not enter. Ask the owner to tie the dog. If unavailable, mark 'Inaccessible (Dog)' and leave.
What should MDC do during heavy rain or: Stop reading to avoid electrocution and damage to devices. Take shelter and resume only when it is dry and safe.
How does MDC manage field work in extrem: Start work very early in the morning, stay hydrated, wear protective caps, and take short breaks in shaded areas.
What should MDC do if threatened by a co: Do not argue or fight back. Leave the premises immediately, ensure personal safety, and report the incident to the SDO and Police.
What safety equipment should MDC carry i: Insulated rubber shoes, WAPDA ID card, a hat for sun protection, and optionally a high-visibility vest.
How does MDC handle a meter in a dangero: If near broken walls, deep water, or unstable structures, do not approach. Zoom in with a camera or report it as 'Inaccessible/Dangerous'.
What if a meter is installed near live w: Maintain a safe distance. Do not touch the meter box or wires. Report the hazardous installation to the line staff for relocation.
What should MDC do in case of a road acc: Seek immediate medical help, inform the supervisor for assistance, and file a departmental incident report for medical coverage.
How does MDC manage field work in flood-: Work is usually suspended by higher authorities. If required, coordinate with rescue teams and NEVER approach submerged electrical poles.
What daily reports does MDC submit to su: Total readings taken, list of unread/locked meters, list of defective meters, and any theft/tampering reports.
What is an MDC monthly report?: A summary of the entire month's route completion percentage, total defects reported, and overall field performance.
How does MDC document inaccessible meter: By selecting the specific reason code (Locked, Dog, Dangerous) in the HHU and capturing a supporting photograph of the obstacle.
What is a DCO (Disconnection Order) and: An order to cut power for non-payment. MDCs may be asked to take a final reading before the line staff disconnect the wire.
What is a reconnection report in WAPDA?: A report confirming that a disconnected consumer has paid dues and the line staff have safely reconnected their power.
How does MDC maintain a consumer ledger?: Modern MDCs don't use paper ledgers; the HHU app serves as a digital ledger. Historical data is viewed directly on the screen.
What is a route book and how does MDC us: A printed backup or reference showing the sequential geographical order of meters to ensure no house is skipped.
What records must MDC keep for audit pur: Photographs of defective meters, signed physical reports submitted to SDO, and route completion logs.
How does MDC handle a discrepancy in rou: If a house is missing or added, they note the details and submit a 'Route Adjustment' request to the Revenue Office.
What is the MIS report in WAPDA MDC work: Management Information System report. It tracks MDC performance, error rates, and reading efficiency across the subdivision.
What are the WAPDA electricity rules rel: Rules regarding right of entry for meter reading, prohibition of tampering, and procedures for issuing detection bills.
What is the NEPRA role in MDC operations: NEPRA sets the tariffs, consumer service manuals, and accuracy standards that WAPDA/DISCOs and MDCs must follow.
What is the DISCO structure MDC works un: Subdivision (headed by SDO) -> Division (headed by XEN) -> Circle (headed by SE) -> DISCO HQ (headed by CEO).
What is LESCO, MEPCO, FESCO and which co: They are regional Distribution Companies. MEPCO (Multan Electric Power Company) covers Khanewal.
What are the conduct rules MDC must foll: Wear official ID, behave politely with consumers, never accept bribes, and perform duties with absolute honesty.
What disciplinary action can be taken ag: For fake readings or corruption, actions range from suspension and salary deduction to termination and legal FIRs.
What is the leave policy for MDC in WAPD: MDCs are entitled to casual leaves, earned leaves, and medical leaves as per standard Pakistan Government service rules.
What is the overtime policy for MDC?: Overtime is rarely applicable for MDCs unless explicitly ordered during special campaigns or emergencies. Usually, compensation is via T.A (Traveling Allowance).
What are the pension and benefits for MD: Permanent MDCs receive medical coverage, electricity unit subsidies, GP fund, and a pension upon retirement.
What is the code of conduct for MDC in t: Maintain professionalism, respect consumer privacy, do not enter homes without permission, and document everything accurately.
How does MDC coordinate with the SDO off: By attending morning briefings, submitting daily defect reports, and escalating field disputes to the SDO.
What is MDC's role during WAPDA load she: MDCs have no role in load shedding. They continue reading meters whenever power is available (or using flashlights on digital meters if off).
How does MDC work with the disconnection: MDCs provide lists of chronically locked or highly suspicious meters for the disconnection team to target.
What is MDC's role during a WAPDA audit?: MDCs assist auditors by providing physical route guidance and confirming the existence and status of specific meters.
How does MDC coordinate with the revenue: By ensuring data is synced before billing deadlines and assisting in correcting any 'unbilled' or 'erroneous' consumer accounts.
What is MDC's role in new connection pro: MDCs may be asked to verify the site, ensure no previous default exists, and take the very first 'Zero' reading of the newly installed meter.
How does MDC assist in tariff revision i: By ensuring all meter readings are completely up-to-date right before a new tariff goes into effect to avoid legal billing issues.
What is MDC's role during a meter change: They record the final reading of the old removed meter and the starting reading of the newly installed meter to ensure billing continuity.
How does MDC work with the complaints ce: They provide on-ground verification (photos and current readings) for complaints registered at the customer service center.
What is MDC's role in WAPDA's loss reduc: They are the frontline; by reporting kunda connections, sluggish meters, and tampered seals, they directly reduce line losses.
What mobile apps does WAPDA MDC use in f: Specific DISCO-issued Android apps (like PITC Meter Reading App) designed for secure login, reading entry, and photo capture.
How does MDC use GPS for route tracking?: The reading app uses the phone's GPS to geo-tag the exact location where the meter reading photograph was taken to prevent 'fake/desktop' readings.
What is the role of MDC in smart meterin: MDCs help map existing manual meters to GIS systems and conduct physical audits of the newly installed smart meters.
How does MDC handle AMI (Advanced Meteri: AMI meters send data automatically. MDCs only visit these for maintenance, physical tamper checks, or if the communication module fails.
What cybersecurity rules apply to MDC da: MDCs must not share their login passwords, must not install third-party apps on official devices, and must secure the device from theft.
How does MDC use camera phones for docum: They take clear, time-stamped, and geo-tagged photos of the meter display to serve as indisputable proof of the reading.
What is the role of MDC in WAPDA's digit: MDCs are the primary data entry point. The accuracy of their field data ensures the success of WAPDA's digital billing and tracking systems.
How does MDC handle system downtime duri: They continue taking readings offline on the app. Once the main server is back online, the app automatically syncs the cached data.
What is a barcode scanner used for by MD: Some apps allow scanning the barcode on the meter to instantly pull up the consumer's record, preventing manual entry errors.
How does cloud data storage affect MDC w: It allows instant, real-time syncing of readings to headquarters, enabling same-day billing and immediate defect tracking.
